_Toc170734955 \h 8 13 Questions and Answers PAGEREF _Toc170734956 \h 9 Enhancement Revision History Changed By Date Version Notes: Co dy Ramangkoun 07 / 01 /202 4 0.0 Original Document EX 9 6 – Add Cross-Reference oLPN Number (XREF / ToteID) to Task Details UI McLane utilizes a tote scan as a XREF_ O LPN identifier when picking in Active Picking areas. As per the EX96 custom flow, WMOS is enhanced to display the user scanned XREF_OLPN on the task detail screen to better identify current scanned totes for an oLPN . Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Task Detail UI MHE N RF N Reporting N Other N WMOS is enhanced to include custom field TASK_DTL.C_XREF_GLP to have XREF/Tote ID added to the TASK_DTL table and Task detail UI upon user scanning . Assumptions EX33 is enabled. GLP/Tote NBR does not get clear ed by WM after tote is in loaded status. EX96 is to work solely for INT50s that use EX33 workflow where XREF/tote scan is prompted and successfully accepted by WM . Dependencies Code Dependencies EX 33 – RF Picking for Active Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement Platform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Process Flow Diagram User enters RF Picking Transaction User Confirm LPN Size Type Screen User is prompted for XREF_OLPN scans. Upon User Entries, the LPN. XREF_OLPN is updated in the DB to TASK_DTL.C_XREF_GLP and in the task details UI. Task Details UI Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX96 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX96 is turned off, and no custom EX9 6 processing will occur. Task Detail U I WMOS is enhanced to include custom field TASK_DTL.C_XREF_GLP to have XREF/ToteID added to the TASK_DTL table and Task detail UI upon user scanning. For chase waving , WMOS validates chase task s are created for short ed oLPNs ( PICKING_SHORT_ ITEM. TC_LPN_ID ) . If a chase task is created ( TASK_DTL .IS_CHASE_CREATED = ‘1’ ) , WMOS is enhanced to prepopulate the TASK_DTL. C_XREF_GLP column of the chase task with the LPN.XREF_OLPN belonging to the shorted oLPN . Cross Reference oLPN Number for Slot ID 1 Cross Reference oLPN Number for Slot ID 2 Display # Literal Source Display Length (R/L justified) 1 ‘ Cross-Reference oLPN Number ’ SELECT XREF_OLPN FROM LPN WHERE TC_LPN_ID = <CURRENT LPN ON TASK DTL> 20 A (L) 2 3 4 5 User Entry N/A User Actions N/A Outputs Activity Tracking No changes to base activity tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to base activity tracking as part of this extension. LM Hooks MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF No RF changes in scope for this extension. Process Change/Addition No additional changes to process Updates No custom updates are made as part of this extension. Outputs Activity Tracking No changes to base behavior for Activity Tracking with the wave allocation as part of this extension PIX No changes to base behavior for PIX with the wave allocation as part of this extension. LM Hooks No changes to base behavior for labor management with the wave allocation as part of this extension. Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label, or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front . Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below. Menu Parameters Parameter (Category > Parameter) Value Warehouse Master Parameter (Tab > Section > Parameter) Value System Codes Code ID Details Parameter Configuration Rec Type Code Type Code ID Short Description Description From To Description Validation Type Configured value C 000 000 Global Extension Control Global Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – Global Extension Control is turned ON C 000 EX96 EX96 Extension Control EX96 Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – EX96 Extension Control is turned ON Database The following table, column combinations are added to the database schema. TABLE NAME COLUMN NAME Nullable Data Type Constrains DB Sequence TASK_DTL C_XREF_GLP Y Varchar( 20) N/A N/A Custom Configurations System Code Types Rec Type Code Type Code Desc RF Desc Max Len.
